content.problemStatement:
Consider an undirected graph GGG with NNN vertices and MMM edges. A line graph L(G)L(G)L(G) of this graph is a graph with MMM vertices; each of these vertices corresponds to an edge in GGG and two vertices in L(G)L(G)L(G) are connected by an edge if the corresponding edges in GGG have a common vertex.

Let Lk(G)L^k(G)Lk(G) denote the result of performing the operation "replace GGG by L(G)L(G)L(G)" kkk times, i.e. L1(G)=L(G)L^1(G) = L(G)L1(G)=L(G), L2(G)=L(L(G))L^2(G) = L(L(G))L2(G)=L(L(G)), L3(G)=L(L(L(G)))L^3(G)=L(L(L(G)))L3(G)=L(L(L(G))) and so on.

You are given an undirected simple graph GGG with NNN vertices (numbered 111 through NNN) and MMM edges, and a positive integer KKK. Find the size of the largest independent set in LK(G)L^K(G)LK(G). Since this value may be very large, compute it modulo 998,244,353998,244,353998,244,353.
constraints: GGG does not contain any self-loops or parallel edges
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains three space-separated integers NNN, MMM and KKK.
Each of the next MMM lines contains two space-separated integers uuu and vvv denoting that vertices uuu and vvv are connected by an edge.
content.outputFormat: Print a single line containing one integer ― the number of vertices in the largest independent set in LK(G)L^K(G)LK(G), modulo 998,244,353998,244,353998,244,353.
